text: The Cambridge Analytica scandal of 2018 offered a rare public glimpse of behavioral prediction markets operating at political scale. The firm harvested personality profiles derived from Facebook data and used them to serve micro-targeted political messages calibrated to individual psychological vulnerabilities — authoritarianism, fear, tribalism. What made this episode significant was not the scale of the manipulation but its visibility. Political advertising has always sought to move behavior; Cambridge Analytica revealed that the infrastructure built by surveillance capitalism made this manipulation orders of magnitude more precise, cheap, and deniable than anything previously available.
question: According to the passage, what made the Cambridge Analytica episode especially significant?

options.text: The unprecedented number of users whose data was harvested from Facebook
Its revelation that surveillance capitalism infrastructure made behavioral manipulation far more precise, cheap, and deniable
The fact that it was the first time personality profiling was used for political advertising
Zuboff's direct involvement in exposing the firm's methodology through academic research
